title: Windows 11 24H2 Home and Pro reach end of support in 90 days
summary: Microsoft announced on Wednesday that systems running Windows 10 Enterprise LTSB 2016 and Home and Pro editions of Windows 11 24H2 will stop receiving updates in three months. [...]

title: CISA orders feds to patch actively exploited Oracle flaw by Saturday
summary: CISA has ordered federal agencies to secure their systems by Saturday against ongoing attacks exploiting a critical vulnerability in the Oracle E-Business Suite financial application. [...]

title: Russian hackers trojanize WebEx, Zoom apps to push Starland malware
summary: A financially motivated Russian threat actor tracked as UAT-11795 is using trojanized software to steal credentials and cryptocurrency by deploying a new backdoor called Starland RAT. [...]

title: New Spirals ransomware encrypts victim network in under 24 hours
summary: A new ransomware actor called Spirals completed a corporate intrusion, from initial access to data theft and encryption, in less than 24 hours. [...]

title: Dutch police bust investment fraud ring stealing over €100 million
summary: The Dutch Police announced the arrest of multiple individuals suspected of being part of an international investment fraud scheme estimated to have tens of thousands of victims. [...]

title: Zoom warns of critical account takeover vulnerability
summary: Zoom is warning of a critical vulnerability in its desktop client and software development kit for Windows that could be exploited by an unauthenticated party to hijack accounts. [...]

title: Google Gemini CLI abused as a hacking agent, malware botnet operator
summary: A Russian-speaking threat actor known as "bandcampro" used Google's open-source Gemini CLI AI tool as a hacking agent and to operate a small-scale botnet. [...]

title: AsyncAPI npm packages infected with credential-stealing malware
summary: Five malicious versions of AsyncAPI packages were published to the Node Package Manager (npm) in a supply-chain attack that delivered a remote access trojan with info-stealing capabilities. [...]

title: We built a vulnerability vending machine: AI tokens in, zero-days out
summary: Intruder built an AI-powered "vulnerability vending machine" that combines code slicing with LLMs to automatically discover complex software vulnerabilities. The company explains how the system found and exploited a previously unknown WordPress plugin zero-day, with additional discoveries already under responsible disclosure. [...]

title: CISA warns admins to patch actively exploited SharePoint flaws
summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) warned Tuesday that attackers are actively exploiting three vulnerabilities to hack Internet-exposed on-premises SharePoint Server instances. [...]

title: Microsoft: Some Dell PCs shut down after recent Windows updates
summary: Microsoft is blocking this month's Windows 11 security updates on some Dell devices because they are causing shutdowns and performance issues. [...]

title: US charges alleged operators of Russian bulletproof hosting service
summary: U.S. federal prosecutors have unsealed charges against three Russian nationals, accusing them of providing bulletproof hosting (BPH) services to ransomware gangs that caused over $62 million in damages to victims worldwide. [...]

title: SonicWall warns of SMA1000 flaws exploited in zero-day attacks, patch now
summary: SonicWall warns that threat actors have been exploiting two SMA1000 vulnerabilities, tracked as CVE-2026-15409 and CVE-2026-15410, in zero-day attacks and urges customers to install the newly released security updates. [...]

title: Spanish Police take down €140 million cyber fraud ring, arrest four
summary: The Spanish Police dismantled a cybercrime and money-laundering organization that made €140 million ($160 million) from investment fraud and business email compromise (BEC) attacks. [...]

title: Nearly 300 GitHub repos pose as legit software to push malware
summary: A threat actor has published hundreds of fake GitHub repositories impersonating legitimate software and security projects to distribute infostealer malware. [...]
